milestones.title: Preliminary Inquiry Leads to Formal Model Re-evaluation
milestones.summary: Sean initiated an inquiry into the NOP Risk Score Model's efficacy, which quickly escalated into a formal proposal for re-evaluation. This marked the beginning of the project due to concerns about model validation and SAS implementation.

milestones.title: Project Scoping and Strategic Directive Issued
milestones.summary: Carolyn and Marc responded to the re-evaluation proposal, leading to project scoping and a strategic directive for NOP Risk Model Modernization. This phase focused on budget neutrality, data hygiene, and establishing project governance.

milestones.title: Consultancy Awarded and Project Kick-off Initiated
milestones.summary: A consultancy was awarded, and Dr. Aris joined the project, leading to the project kick-off and definition of initial data requirements. This phase focused on expert profiling, data dictionaries, and aligning stakeholders.

milestones.title: Data Requirements Defined and Algorithm Methodology Drafted
milestones.summary: The project team defined data requirements and Dr. Aris drafted the initial algorithm methodology. This involved discussions on data hygiene, legacy decay, and geographic weighting.

milestones.title: Correlation Results and Field Perspective Concerns Emerge
milestones.summary: Preliminary correlation results were shared, and concerns from the field perspective regarding the draft risk model arose. This led to discussions on geographic weighting, regional safety, and sensitivity analysis.

milestones.title: Decision on Model Weighting and Final Sign-Off
milestones.summary: A critical project review resulted in a decision on model weighting, balancing inspector feedback and model precision. This led to a final sign-off on the model's parameters.

milestones.title: Implementation and Final Model Documentation Delivered
milestones.summary: Implementation of low-risk streamlining in SAS was initiated, and the final model documentation and methodology report were delivered. This marked the completion of the model development phase.

milestones.title: Conflict Check and Ontario Pilot Results
milestones.summary: A conflict check report was generated, and the Ontario pilot results for the new risk model were analyzed. This provided initial insights into the model's performance in a real-world setting.

milestones.title: Formal Request and Approval for National Roll-out
milestones.summary: Carolyn formally requested a national roll-out of the TDG Risk Model for 2026-27, which was subsequently approved by Marc. This signified the successful completion of the development and testing phases.

milestones.title: Project Wrap-up and Next Steps Defined
milestones.summary: With the national roll-out approved, Sean outlined the project wrap-up activities and defined the next steps. This included ongoing data monitoring and preparation for audits.

milestones.title: Post-Launch Monitoring and Audit Committee Feedback
milestones.summary: Post-launch data monitoring was conducted, and the project team prepared for the Fall Audit Committee. Positive feedback was received from the Audit Committee, confirming the project's success.

milestones.title: Project Closeout and Viya Kickoff
milestones.summary: The project officially closed out, with a focus on knowledge transfer and documentation. A kickoff meeting was also held to discuss the transition to SAS Viya.

milestones.title: Audit Closed with No Findings Reported
milestones.summary: The internal audit was successfully closed with no findings reported, validating the integrity and compliance of the modernized risk model.

milestones.title: Q2 Model Recalibration and Performance Metrics Finalized
milestones.summary: The team initiated Q2 model recalibration due to shifts in Class 3 transport trends. The FY2026 performance metrics were finalized, demonstrating the ongoing monitoring and refinement of the model.

milestones.title: Project Handover and New Directions
milestones.summary: The project transitioned to 'Business as Usual' (BAU) and a handover session was held in preparation for the integration of the AI Agent (GuardClaw). This marked the end of the modernization project and the beginning of a new phase of oversight.
